sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Applications 412 to 416 of 2022 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection(s). 2. For orders on CMA 473/2022. 3. For hearing of Main Case. 09.12.2025 Mr. Faheem Ali Memon, advocate for the applicant. Mr. Muhammad Din Qazi, advocate files power of Mr. Muhammad Faheem Bhayo, advocate for the respondent in all captioned references, respectively, which are taken on record. Pursuant to order dated 11.11.2025, learned counsel for the respondent states that it may be just and proper to set-aside the impugned judgment and remand the matter back to the learned Tribunal for adjudication afresh expeditiously, preferably, within 90 days. He seeks that pending the aforesaid, no coercive action be taken against the respondent arising herefrom. Learned counsel for the applicant articulates no cavil in such regard and states that this reference may be disposed of in the same terms. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office to place copy hereof in the connected matters. Judge Judge M. Khan
